method: Multi-source code evidence extraction followed by editorial reconciliation. Shared frequency established candidates; semantic role and repeated public-page use determined the final style.

sources.name: fooling
observations: Playfair Display headlines, Source Serif 4 reading text, and Inter utility text have explicit roles.
A twelve-column image-led lead story, compact side rail, four-column story grids, and max-width reading column form the editorial hierarchy.
Deep navy signal surfaces, warm near-white content surfaces, red category emphasis, thin rules, and a 6px base radius create urgency without visual noise.

sources.name: story-fooling
observations: The same three-family typography split is declared in both Tailwind and the loaded web fonts.
Warm paper surfaces, near-black ink, red editorial accents, 65ch prose, section rules, drop caps, and restrained hover lift support long-form reading.
Ticker and discovery surfaces use sans-serif uppercase labels while narrative titles and body copy retain serif hierarchy.

sharedDecisions.decision: Use Playfair Display for story identity, Source Serif 4 for sustained reading, and Inter only for navigation, metadata, labels, and controls.
sharedDecisions.reason: This role split is explicit in both repositories and visible across their public layouts.

sharedDecisions.decision: Treat red as an editorial signal, not a general decoration or second background system.
sharedDecisions.reason: Both sources reserve red for categories, active states, live or breaking signals, and concise calls to action.

sharedDecisions.decision: Build hierarchy with typography, image scale, grid span, and thin rules before cards, shadows, or decorative containers.
sharedDecisions.reason: Both public experiences are publication-like and remain scannable without dashboard-style card chrome.

sharedDecisions.decision: Keep corners small and elevation exceptional.
sharedDecisions.reason: Source components use modest rounding; shadows appear mainly on image overlays, hover emphasis, or overlays rather than every content block.

reconciledDivergences.difference: Fooling uses a deeper navy semantic system; Story Fooling uses a warmer paper-and-ink system.
reconciledDivergences.resolution: Warm paper is the default reading surface; deep navy is retained for ticker, masthead, hero, and other high-signal bands.

reconciledDivergences.difference: Story Fooling contains a dense, system-sans administration UI and specialized graph palettes.
reconciledDivergences.resolution: Excluded because it belongs to an operational product surface, not the shared public editorial language.
